channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Spurs needed 'complete reset', says under fire CEO
item.description: In a wide-ranging exclusive interview with BBC Sport, Tottenham chief executive Vinai Venkatesham talks about a number of issues the club needs to address.

item.title: 60 Palace 'troublemakers' ordered to leave Leipzig
item.description: Police say 60 Crystal Palace fans classified as "known troublemakers" have been ordered to leave Leipzig while two arrests were made before the Europa Conference League final.

item.title: The footballer setting record straight after 46 years
item.description: Roly Gregoire, Sunderland's first black player, sometimes wishes he had never played football because of the racist abuse he found too painful to talk about for 46 years.

item.title: Northern Ireland boss O'Neill signs four-year extension
item.description: Northern Ireland manager Michael O'Neill has signed a four-year contract extension to keep him at the helm until 2032.

item.title: Will 'bruised' Howe and Newcastle address problems in one window?
item.description: Newcastle certainly have to get this summer right following a turbulent window a year ago.

item.title: 'Beginning of era' for young Barcelona team - Deco
item.description: After back-to-back La Liga titles, sporting director Deco says a new era is only just beginning at La Liga champions Barcelona.

item.title: Foden a 'victim' of football's packed schedule
item.description: Manchester City midfielder Phil Foden is a "victim" of football's packed fixture schedule after being left out of England's World Cup squad, says the chief executive of the PFA.

item.title: Have Rangers just signed Scotland's World Cup number nine?
item.description: Will Lawrence Shankland's transfer from Heart of Midlothian to Rangers coincide with him becoming Scotland's main striker at the World Cup?

item.title: Man City ahead of Man Utd in race for Anderson
item.description: Manchester City are leading the race to sign Elliot Anderson, with the Nottingham Forest midfielder preferring a move to Etihad Stadium instead of rivals Manchester United.

item.title: London City pursuing Putellas after Barca exit
item.description: Two-time Ballon d'Or winner Alexia Putellas has announced she is leaving Barcelona after 14 years, with London City Lionesses in pursuit.

item.title: Arsenal target Villa's Rogers - Wednesday's gossip
item.description: Arsenal ready to target Morgan Rogers, Paris St-Germain to rival Liverpool for Yan Diomande and Jack Grealish to be given chance to revive Man City career.

item.title: Quiz: Name every Conference League finalist
item.description: Crystal Palace are in Wednesday's Conference League final and are one match away from their first ever European trophy.

item.title: The Sunderland footballer setting record straight after 46 years
item.description: When Roly Gregoire quit football in 1980 after suffering a knee injury, he had faced so much racism that he could not bear to watch football for many years.

item.title: 24 Hours with Ella Toone coming soon
item.description: Watch the trailer for a new BBC Sport documentary offering unprecedented behind-the-scenes access to Manchester United and England star Ella Toone as she prepares for her wedding, while still coming to terms with the loss of her father.

item.title: Spurs secure Premier League survival with win over Everton
item.description: Joao Palhinha's goal just before half time helps Tottenham overcome Everton to secure the result they needed to ensure Premier League survival on a day of nerve-shredding tension.

item.title: West Ham relegated despite win over Leeds
item.description: West Ham's 14-year stay in the Premier League comes to an end after they were relegated to the Championship on the final day of the season despite beating Leeds at London Stadium.

item.title: Arsenal beat Palace before lifting Premier League trophy
item.description: Arsenal start their reign as Premier League champions with a routine win over Crystal Palace in a party atmosphere at Selhurst Park.

item.title: The 2025-26 Premier League season in alternative tables
item.description: BBC Sport presents some alternative Premier League tables, looking at set-pieces, xG, long shots, and more.

item.title: 'Long time coming' as Shankland joins Rangers from Hearts
item.description: Scotland striker Lawrence Shankland says he is fulfilling his "dream" of joining "boyhood club" Rangers as he completes his transfer from Scottish Premiership rivals Heart of Midlothian.

item.title: Coventry's Lampard named LMA manager of the year
item.description: Frank Lampard is named the League Managers Association manager of the year after guiding Coventry back to the Premier League.

item.title: Vardy leaves Cremonese but wants to continue career
item.description: Former Leicester striker Jamie Vardy has decided to leave Cremonese when his contract expires this summer.

item.title: Instagram betting ads featuring Kane and Haaland banned
item.description: The advertising watchdog said the adverts featuring top footballers had a strong appeal to under-18s.

item.title: Adams & Robinson in US squad for World Cup
item.description: Mauricio Pochettino includes Bournemouth midfielder Tyler Adams and Fulham left-back Antonee Robinson in his USA squad for the World Cup.

item.title: The big VAR debrief - did it deny Bournemouth Champions League spot?
item.description: BBC Sport assesses how VAR interventions may have impacted games at key moments and how some teams might appreciate the system more than others.

item.title: Sunderland beat Chelsea to qualify for Europa League
item.description: Sunderland beat Chelsea to qualify for European competition for the first time in 53 years, securing a place in the Europa League at the visitors' expense.

item.title: Fernandes breaks Premier League record as Man Utd beat Brighton
item.description: Manchester United beat Brighton on the final day of the season, with captain Bruno Fernandes breaking the Premier League record for most assists in a single campaign.

item.title: Watkins & Villa spoil Guardiola's final Man City match
item.description: Pep Guardiola's 593rd and final game in charge of Manchester City ended in an emotional Premier League defeat as Ollie Watkins scored a double for Aston Villa.

item.title: Liverpool confirm Champions League spot with draw against Brentford
item.description: Liverpool confirm Champions League football for next season despite being held by Brentford as Anfield waved farewell to club legends Mohamed Salah and Andy Robertson.

item.title: Gibbs-White sends England message as Forest draw with Bournemouth
item.description: Nottingham Forest's Morgan Gibbs-White sends a message to England boss Thomas Tuchel, as Bournemouth miss out on the Champions League.

item.title: Fulham finish season with comfortable win over Newcastle
item.description: Substitute Thomas Cairney scores a superb long-range strike as Fulham signed off with a comfortable win against Newcastle United on the final day of the Premier League season.

item.title: Burnley and Wolves end Premier League campaigns with draw
item.description: Burnley and Wolves play out a 1-1 draw at Turf Moor to close out a miserable Premier League campaign for the two sides, who were relegated well in advance of the final day.

item.title: Watch: Arsenal lift Premier League trophy
item.description: Watch Arsenal lift the Premier League trophy as champions of the 2025-26 season at Selhurst Park following their win over Crystal Palace.

item.title: How 'electric' Watkins forced his way back into Tuchel's England
item.description: Match of the Day pundits Alan Shearer and Wayne Rooney praise Aston Villa's Ollie Watkins on his "unbelievable" hot streak since he was left out of the England squad in March.

item.title: Why reluctant 'guru' says Dutch will win World Cup
item.description: Economist Joachim Klement developed a forecast model which has kept a 100% record of predicting World Cup winners since 2014 - who does he think will win in 2026?

item.title: Players won't be stopped from taking stand - Collins on Israel fixtures
item.description: Republic of Ireland captain Nathan Collins says no member of the squad will be held back if they wanted to "take a stand" against the team's Nations League fixtures with Israel later this year.

item.title: Southampton pay tribute as Udoh dies aged 21
item.description: Southampton FC and Royal Antwerp announce the death of former academy player Victor Udoh at the age of 21.

item.title: Rated - Liverpool's six signings from record summer spree
item.description: BBC Sport assesses the impact made by Liverpool's six signings last summer that totalled a record £415m.

item.title: PSG's Hakimi in Morocco squad despite injury
item.description: Morocco captain Achraf Hakimi is named in the Atlas Lions' World Cup squad despite the injury he sustained in the Champions League semi-finals against Bayern Munich.

item.title: Partey in preliminary Ghana squad for World Cup
item.description: Former Arsenal midfielder Thomas Partey has been named in Ghana's preliminary squad for the World Cup.

item.title: 'He showed why good managers need time' - who are this season's over-achievers?
item.description: Tony Pulis explains which Premier League managers have impressed him this season and, down the divisions, who else stands out.

item.title: How Toone is navigating grief through football
item.description: England and Manchester United midfielder Ella Toone opens up about grief, football and preparing for her wedding without her dad in a new BBC documentary.

item.title: DR Congo seek World Cup ticket refunds after Ebola outbreak
item.description: DR Congo fans hoping to watch their side's return to the World Cup face a major hurdle as the US imposes travel restrictions following the latest Ebola outbreak.

item.title: More than 200 managers & coaches lost jobs this season, says LMA
item.description: Over 200 managers and coaches lost their jobs last season in English football in a "hire and fire culture" which "undermines the profession", says League Manager's Association (LMA) chief Richard Bevan

item.title: Premier League quiz: Name every title-winning manager
item.description: Pep Guardiola leaves the Premier League as the division's second-most successful manager.

item.title: Norwich's Cordoba in Panama's World Cup squad
item.description: Norwich City defender Jose Cordoba is the only UK-based player in Panama's squad for the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: World Cup: Every squad as they are announced
item.description: Every squad for the 2026 World Cup in the US, Canada and Mexico as they are announced.

item.title: Premier League predictions - how accurate were BBC Sport pundits?
item.description: Last summer, 33 BBC pundits - plus AI and Opta's 'supercomputer' were asked to pick which teams would finish in the Premier League's top four, and in which order. How did they get on?

item.title: Could title win start period of dominance for Arsenal?
item.description: It's been a long wait but after 22 years, Arsenal finally have their hands on the Premier League trophy once more.

item.title: 'Spurs avoid humiliation but elation of fans will soon turn to anger'
item.description: Tottenham Hotspur's celebrations in survival cannot cover up their embarrassment of a season that almost ended in the unthinkable, says chief football writer Phil McNulty.

item.title: Saying goodbye to Football Focus after 52 years
item.description: After Football Focus broadcasts its final episode after 52 years on the air, former presenters and pundits reminisce about the good times.

item.title: All done deals since February, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: Which club received most red cards in a Premier League season?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything looks at the teams who have received the most red cards in a Premier League season.

item.title: Who won the Premier League golden boot?
item.description: BBC's Ask Me Anything team explains who has won the Premier League golden boot and other individual awards.

item.title: What would Crystal Palace winning Conference League mean for English clubs in Europe?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains how Crystal Palace winning the Conference League would effect English teams entering Europe next season

item.title: Who are the oldest players to feature at a World Cup?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at who the oldest players are in the history of the World Cup

item.title: Who has qualified for next season's Champions League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains which teams have already qualified for the 2026-27 edition of the Champions League.

item.title: Who has the most Premier League assists in a season?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team look at who has provided the most assists in the history of the Premier League.

item.title: What is financial gap between Old Firm and rest of Scottish Premiership?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team look at the financial gap between the Old Firm and the rest of Scottish top-flight football.

item.title: Celtic join chase for striker Rodriguez - gossip
item.description: Celtic keen on La Liga striker as in-demand Kilmarnock midfielder opts to join English Championship newcomers...

item.title: McTominay backs 'great man' Clarke for Scotland stay
item.description: Scotland midfielder Scott McTominay "would love that" if Steve Clarke agrees a new contract to extend his stay as head coach beyond this summer's World Cup.

item.title: 'Outstanding leader' Shinnie's exit to Caley Thistle confirmed
item.description: Inverness Caledonian Thistle confirm their signing of Aberdeen's Scottish Cup-winning captain, Graeme Shinnie.

item.title: Clark makes dad assistant in first management job at Queen of the South
item.description: Nicky Clark appoints his father Sandy as an assistant as the former Rangers and Dundee United striker starts his first management job with Queen of the South.

item.title: Shaw signs new Man City deal after contract U-turn
item.description: Man City striker Khadija Shaw signs a new four-year contract with the club, just days after it looked like she would be leaving the WSL champions this summer.

item.title: How did each WSL club do in 2025-26 season?
item.description: Who were the surprise package in the Women's Super League this season? And who failed to meet expectations?

item.title: From amateurs to SWPL champions - how Olid transformed Hearts
item.description: Eva Olid led Hearts to the Scottish Women's Premier League title, but she departs now after a five-year transformation of the team.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.
